findings.criterion: Then the price is visible in the cart UI at selector #div1 .box3

evidence.quote: assert any(expected_price in price for price in prices), f"Expected price '{expected_price}' not found in cart display"
the product title appears in .box2, the unit price in .box3, and the quantity in .box1.

findings.reasoning: The test locates elements using the exact selector "#div1 .box3" and asserts that an expected price string appears in the element text. This is a concrete DOM text assertion (dom_observation) that directly checks the unit price presentation in the specified element, making the oracle strong for this observable.

findings.criterion: Then the cart display shows product title "The Essence of JavaScript" in #div1 .box2

evidence.quote: assert any(expected_title in title for title in titles), f"Expected title '{expected_title}' not found in cart display"
expression: "any((expected_title in title for title in titles))"

findings.reasoning: The step directly reads text from elements matched by "#div1 .box2" and asserts the expected title is present. This dom_observation is a direct value check, so the oracle is strong for the title observable.

findings.criterion: Then the quantity "1" is visible in the cart UI at selector #div1 .box1

evidence.quote: assert any(expected_quantity in quantity for quantity in quantities), f"Expected quantity '{expected_quantity}' not found in cart display"
expression: "any((expected_quantity in quantity for quantity in quantities))"

findings.reasoning: The test queries elements using the exact selector "#div1 .box1" and asserts the expected quantity string is present in their text. This is a direct DOM text assertion (dom_observation) and therefore constitutes a strong oracle for quantity.
